reason: Mis-attributed provenance for 'Phi(I)=I'. Node 1.1 states 'by GT-positive-unital, Phi(I)=I', citing external GT-positive-unital (Idel 2013, refs/idel-2013/idel-2013.md). But Phi(I)=I is NOT a theorem derivable from Idel; it is a DEFINITIONAL hypothesis of this proof, captured by 'def positive-unital-map' (Phi: B(H)_sa -> B(H)_sa real-linear, positive, unital with Phi(I)=I). Idel's Definition 2.1 (idel-2013.md:345-349) defines positivity/unitality for COMPLEX-linear maps T: M_d -> M_{d'} between matrix algebras -- a different type than the real-linear Phi: B(H)_sa -> B(H)_sa here -- and is a DEFINITION, not a structure theorem about a given Phi. Byte-check confirms the external is itself partly false-provenanced: its source description claims Idel shows 'a unital positive map on B(H)_sa is order-unit-norm contractive (||Phi||=1)', yet Idel contains ZERO occurrences of 'order unit'/'order norm'/'contractive' (grep -c = 0) and never states ||Phi||=1 (verified at idel-2013.md:345-349 and full-file grep). This is the IDENTICAL defect already adjudicated as a valid major challenge on sibling node 1.6 (ch-79721599b24e2763, resolved by re-citing def positive-unital-map) and on dependency node 1.1.1 (ch-d972d56781a4d91c, resolved the same way). Both 1.6 and 1.1.1 were corrected to 'by hypothesis (def positive-unital-map)'; sibling node 1.4 likewise correctly states 'Phi(I)=I by hypothesis (def positive-unital-map)'. Node 1.1 was left uncorrected: its v0->v1 amendment moved ||Phi||=1 into node 1.1.1 but kept the Phi(I)=I attribution pointing at GT-positive-unital. Under the project rule of strict provenance, Phi(I)=I must be cited to def positive-unital-map (by hypothesis), not GT-positive-unital. Fix: replace 'by GT-positive-unital, Phi(I)=I' with 'Phi(I)=I by hypothesis (def positive-unital-map)', matching siblings 1.4/1.6 and dependency 1.1.1.
